content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcer- PRACTICBs C oMMISSIoN
P.O. Box 807 o 428 J Street o Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
November 75,2001
William W. Wynder, Crty Attorney
Burke, Williams & Sorensen,LLP
i8301 Von Karman Avenue, Suite 1050
Irvine, CA92612-1009
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-01-235
Dear Mr. Wynder:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1
On October 15, 1999, you requested advice on behalf of Councilmember Neil
Roth regarding possible conflicts of interest in decisions relating to expansion of the
Civic Center and improvements along Hawthorne Boulevard, major components of
ongoing redevelopment in the City of Lawndale. On December 9, 1999 we declined to
provide written advice on the ground that your inquiries involved past conduct by
Councilmember Roth. (Our file No. W-99-275, citing Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) ) On
July 9, 2OOI, you again sought written advice on behalf of Councilmember Roth, and on
behalf of Mayor Harold Hoffrnan, concerning the same ongoing projects. On August 14,
2001 we again declined to analyze possible conflicts in Councilmember Roth, because of
past conduct, but answered one of your questions concerning a possible conflict of
interest in Mayor Hoffrnan. We also addressed the possibility of participation by both
officials if, notwithstanding any conflicts of interest, their participation was "legally
required" to make up a quorum of the council. (Wynder Advice Letter, No. A-01-166.)
Your present request seeks advice on conflicts of interest in decisions on ten
separate projects. The first two are components of the Civic Center expansion, six others
are related to the Hawthorne Boulevard project, and two are of unclear affrliation, though
already funded. You ask for this advice on behalf of the entire city council/redevelopment
agency; in addition to Councilmember Roth and Mayor Hoffrnan, you now inquire on
behalf of Councilmembers Nancy McKee, Larry Rudolph, and Virginia Rhodes.
1 Gorrernrnent Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.


File No. 0l-235
Page No. 2
We are unable to provide the advice you seek. Your account of the various
projects indicates that numerous decisions have already been made on each project, and
that these projects are closely interrelated. At the same time, you do not describe in detail
any specific upcoming decisions. Conflicts of interest exist only in connection with
specific decisions. Without sufficient information on particular decisions, questions
relating to potential conflicts of interest are hypothetical, and the Commission may
decline to provide formal advice or informal assistance in response to such questions.
(Regulations 1 83 2e(b)(s)(C), @); rl32e(cX+Xo) )
As we explained in our two prior letters, the Commission may also decline to
provide formal advice or informal assistance when an inquiry involves past conduct by
the officials in question. (Regulations 18329(b)(sXA); 18329(Q@)(A) ) your request
would necessarily require at least implicit commentary on the conduct of all five officials
in prior decisions. We therefore decline to provide either formal advice or informal
assistance in connection with your question on possible conflicts of interest in decisions
relating to the projects you describe.
You also asked about potential application of the "public generally" exception, if
a particular offrcial were found to have a conflict of interest in any of these decisions.
You have not provided sufficient information for us to advise you on the potential
application of the "public generally exception" to the conflict of interest provisions.
Further, it would be difficult to perform this analysis without addressing past conduct.
If you believe our understanding of the facts is incorrect, or if you have
additional facts that make your questions more susceptible to prospective advice, please
feel free to contact us at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counselfurul"!//L
By: Lawrence T. Woodlock
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
'10
'Jb

sections.conclusion: We are unable to provide the advice you seek. Your account of the various projects indicates that numerous decisions have already been made on each project, and that these projects are closely interrelated. At the same time, you do not describe in detail any specific upcoming decisions. Conflicts of interest exist only in connection with specific decisions. Without sufficient information on particular decisions, questions relating to potential conflicts of interest are hypothetical, and the Commission may decline to provide formal advice or informal assistance in response to such questions. As we explained in our two prior letters, the Commission may also decline to provide formal advice or informal assistance when an inquiry involves past conduct by the officials in question. Your request would necessarily require at least implicit commentary on the conduct of all five officials in prior decisions. We therefore decline to provide either formal advice or informal assistance in connection with your question on possible conflicts of interest in decisions relating to the projects you describe.
sections.question_synthetic: Do Councilmembers Neil Roth, Harold Hoffman, Nancy McKee, Larry Rudolph, and Virginia Rhodes have conflicts of interest in decisions relating to ten separate redevelopment projects in the City of Lawndale, and does the 'public generally' exception apply to any such conflicts?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the inquiry involves past conduct by the officials and lacks sufficient detail about specific prospective decisions, making the questions hypothetical. The request also involves the 'public generally' exception but insufficient information was provided to analyze it without addressing past conduct.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with explicit CONCLUSION section. Document references prior declinations and a prior advice letter (A-01-166). No traditional QUESTION section header but inquiry clearly stated in body text. File number W-01-235, dated November 15, 2001.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Do Councilmembers Neil Roth, Harold Hoffman, Nancy McKee, Larry Rudolph, and Virginia Rhodes have conflicts of interest in decisions relating to ten separate redevelopment projects in the City of Lawndale, and does the 'public generally' exception apply to any such conflicts?

CONCLUSION: We are unable to provide the advice you seek. Your account of the various projects indicates that numerous decisions have already been made on each project, and that these projects are closely interrelated. At the same time, you do not describe in detail any specific upcoming decisions. Conflicts of interest exist only in connection with specific decisions. Without sufficient information on particular decisions, questions relating to potential conflicts of interest are hypothetical, and the Commission may decline to provide formal advice or informal assistance in response to such questions. As we explained in our two prior letters, the Commission may also decline to provide formal advice or informal assistance when an inquiry involves past conduct by the officials in question. Your request would necessarily require at least implicit commentary on the conduct of all five officials in prior decisions. We therefore decline to provide either formal advice or informal assistance in connection with your question on possible conflicts of interest in decisions relating to the projects you describe.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide written advice on potential conflicts of interest for five Lawndale city officials regarding ten redevelopment projects, citing lack of specificity regarding future decisions and the involvement of past conduct by the officials.
